commit.message:
fix(keyring): add hardware marker to prevent software fallback

When a key is secured by hardware (Android Keystore, Secure Enclave,
TPM), the software signer now has a marker set that prevents fallback
to a wrong key if hardware access fails transiently.

Changes:
- Add hardware_key_marker field to Ed25519SoftwareSigner
- Set marker on hardware import/load instead of copying key to software
- get_public_key() and sign() return error when marker set but HW fails
- Add retry logic with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 50ms→100ms→200ms)
- Add generate_key() method that uses hardware when available
- Update FFI to use generate_key() for auto-generation

This fixes the bug where portal key import worked, but 10 seconds later
a different local key was returned due to incorrect software fallback.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Huma query param pointer panic in pending shows endpoint (PSY-83)

Huma v2 does not support pointer types (*uint, *string) for
query/path/header parameters — panics at route registration.
Use value types with zero-value semantics instead.

Closes PSY-83

commit.message:
Add Geist Pixel font with split-flap animations and choreographed hero entrance

Integrates Geist Pixel Square for "interaction delight" in two strategic spots:
the hero name and closing CTA tagline. Characters cycle through random pixel
glyphs then cross-dissolve to sans, conveying the hybrid coder/designer identity.
Hero entrance is fully choreographed (photo → label → split-flap → desc → icons
→ sections) for a cohesive load experience. CTA includes proximity-based hover
that flips characters back to pixel font near the cursor.

Closes #14

commit.message:
fix(modules): export struct and export enum fully supported — H-181, H-185

- Parser now accepts `export struct` and `export enum` (AT1000 gone)
- AST: ExportItem gains Struct(StructDecl) and Enum(EnumDecl) variants
- SymbolTable: struct_exports and enum_exports maps, imported via binder
- Binder: named imports resolve struct/enum from source module's symbol table
- TypeChecker: collect_struct_decls and collect_enum_names walk Export items
  and inject from symbol_table (imported types visible to type resolution)
- Interpreter: module type-only exports (struct/enum/type alias) skipped at
  runtime with new module_type_exports_cache; bind_imports handles type-only
- ModuleExecutor: same fix — type-only names cached separately, no AT5004
- ModuleLoader: struct/enum names included in export name list
- Formatter + LSP (index, symbols): all ExportItem match sites updated
- 5 TDD tests all GREEN

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: bump @mctx-ai/mcp-server to 0.8.0 (#49)

## Why

The `@mctx-ai/mcp-server` framework released 0.8.0, which removed the
env third parameter from handler signatures that was briefly introduced
in 0.7.0. The example server should track the latest framework version
so developers see current patterns.

## What This Does

Bumps the framework dependency to `^0.8.0` and updates the `greet` tool
handler to use the straightforward `process.env` lazy loading pattern —
reading env vars inside the handler body (not at module scope) for
runtime compatibility.

---
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: uniform Y-axis decimal places per chart

yTickFmt() inspects all tick values, finds the max decimal places
needed (capped at 2), then formats every tick to that same precision.
e.g. if ticks are 3.25/3.3/3.35 → max is 2 → all show as 3.25/3.30/3.35
e.g. if ticks are 12.8/13/13.2 → max is 1 → all show as 12.8/13.0/13.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Mobile: fix all-Unclassified bug — use sortedCats() instead of is_active filter

CategoryResponse omits is_active, so c.is_active was undefined (falsy) in the
frontend. The mobile filter categories.filter(c=>c.is_active) returned [] for
every render, leaving only the hardcoded <option value="">Unclassified</option>.
Now uses the global sortedCats() helper, consistent with desktop TxnRow.

commit.message:
Separate diagram generation into async Gemini-powered endpoint (#11)

The architecture generator was slow because it ran two sequential Claude
API calls in one SSE stream. This splits diagram generation into a
separate /api/generate-diagram endpoint using Gemini 2.0 Flash, so the
main architecture result (tools, summary, build steps, tradeoffs) returns
after just one Claude call. The diagram loads asynchronously afterward.

- Add @google/genai dependency and GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY env var
- Create /api/generate-diagram with Gemini Flash + mermaid validation/repair
- Remove Stage 2, repairDiagram, and MERMAID_RULES from /api/generate
- Update SSE types: diagram -> diagramDescription in result payload
- Simplify GenerateStatus (remove diagram-related stages)
- Auto-fetch diagram in architecture-generator with loading skeleton
- Disable Share button until diagram is ready

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: book buttons + ?book=1 now open ETHEREAL chat instead of legacy BookingWizard

All booking CTAs (navigation, sticky bar, exit intent, ?book=1 URL param)
now open the ETHEREAL ChatEmbed widget via window.EtherealChat.open().
Legacy BookingWizard kept as fallback only if widget fails to load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add pipeline orchestrator and admin trigger endpoints (PSY-79)

Tie together fetcher, extraction, and import into an end-to-end AI
extraction pipeline. The PipelineService orchestrates: load venue
source config -> fetch page (static/dynamic/screenshot) -> check for
changes -> auto-detect render method if needed -> extract events via
AI -> convert to DiscoveredEvent format -> import via DiscoveryService
-> record run and update config.

New admin endpoints:
- POST /admin/pipeline/extract/{venue_id}?dry_run=true - trigger
  extraction for a single venue with optional preview mode
- GET /admin/pipeline/venues - list all configured venues with source
  configs and last extraction run info

20 service unit tests covering success, dry run, no config, no
calendar URL, unchanged page skip, auto-detection, extraction failure,
fetch failure, unknown render method, import failure (non-fatal),
empty results, and all three render methods. 10 handler unit tests
covering admin guard, extract, dry run, invalid ID, service error,
list venues, empty list, and service error.

Closes PSY-79

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: trim all Shopify env vars to prevent auth failures

Trailing whitespace/newlines in SHOPIFY_API_SECRET cause JWT signature
verification to fail, resulting in 401 on all API calls. Trim all
env vars (apiKey, apiSecretKey, scopes, hostName) during initialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

prom/changes (#14)

* fix: formatting tool result

* fix(prompt): clean up prompt construction pipeline

- Stop wrapping prompt layers with redundant `# TITLE` header since
  files already contain their own markdown headers
- Store clean display name (username only) in message history instead of
  verbose "username (id:xxx)" format
- Remove score metadata from preinjected memory labels to reduce noise

  rename section from CURRENT SPEAKER to CURRENT CONTEXT

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(fusion): decompose monolithic fusion_engine.py into fusion/ package

4-phase MMFE refactoring based on technical review:

Phase 1 — Engineering decoupling:
- Split 2266-line fusion_engine.py into 22 focused modules under fusion/
- 14 core modules: models, constants, io, raster_utils, profiling, matching,
  compatibility, alignment, execution, validation, db, llm_routing,
  schema_alignment
- 11 strategy files under fusion/strategies/ (10 strategies + registry)
- Thin proxy fusion_engine.py preserves all existing imports and mock targets
- Updated 14 test patch targets to point to actual sub-modules

Phase 2 — AI logic refinement:
- Deprecated LLM strategy routing (llm_auto → rule-based auto with warning)
- Added user_hint keyword heuristic to _score_strategies (13 mappings)
- New schema_alignment.py: LLM-based schema alignment via Gemini 2.0 Flash
- Integrated use_llm_schema parameter through matching → compatibility → tools

Phase 3 — Async event loop unblocking:
- Converted 4 fusion tool functions to async def
- Heavy compute wrapped in asyncio.to_thread() to prevent ASGI GIL lockup
- Input validation stays on main thread

Phase 4 — PostGIS compute push-down:
- Extended FusionSource with postgis_table/postgis_srid fields
- New postgis_pushdown.py: 3 SQL strategies (spatial_join, overlay, nearest)
- SQL injection prevention via table name regex whitelist
- Automatic fallback to Python on push-down failure
- New profile_postgis_source() for PostGIS table profiling

All 130 fusion tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix deploy workflow ordering and push robustness (#16)

* Fix deploy workflow: create release before pushing updates.json

Move GitHub Release creation before the git push step so the XPI
artifact is always uploaded even if the push fails. Also add
`git pull --rebase` before pushing to handle commits that land on
main between workflow start and the push step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix expression injection in Create GitHub Release step

Pass github.ref_name and step outputs through env vars instead of
interpolating them directly into the shell string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add chromedp rendering for dynamic and screenshot fetching (PSY-78)

Extend FetcherService with chromedp-based rendering for JS-heavy venue sites:
- FetchDynamic: headless Chrome renders page, extracts DOM HTML
- FetchScreenshot: full-page screenshot as base64 (JPEG)
- DetectRenderMethod: auto-detect static/dynamic/screenshot tier
- Worker pool with configurable concurrency (default 3)
- Graceful shutdown via ShutdownChromedp()
- Tests skip gracefully when Chrome is not installed

Closes PSY-78

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: prefer idToken() over stale URL token for API calls

Now that idToken() works, use it as the primary token source since it
returns fresh tokens. The URL id_token expires quickly and was causing
401s on API calls like /api/seed. URL token is now only a fallback
for initial auth before App Bridge loads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Shop-assistant
repository.description: assistant

commit.message:
fix: check existing session before JWT verification in token exchange

Decode JWT payload without signature verification first to get shop
domain, then check for existing session before attempting full
token exchange. Prevents 500 errors from expired/invalid URL tokens
when a session already exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: trim API key env var to remove trailing newline

NEXT_PUBLIC_SHOPIFY_API_KEY had a trailing \n in Vercel, causing
App Bridge to fail matching the API key. This made idToken() and
resourcePicker() hang. Trim in code as a safety net.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add progress monitoring to data loader

- Read all lines upfront to show total document count
- Log progress every 50 docs: [current/total] chunks new/updated/skipped elapsed
- Final summary includes total docs, total chunks, per-status counts, elapsed time

Example output:
  총 3,241개 문서 로드 시작.
  [50/3241] 청크: 312 (new 45 | updated 3 | skipped 264) | 경과: 0:00:42
  [100/3241] 청크: 623 (new 89 | updated 6 | skipped 528) | 경과: 0:01:18
  완료 | 문서: 3,241/3,241 | 청크: 19,847 (new 412 | updated 28 | skipped 19,407 | failed 0) | 총 소요: 0:42:11

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: mycomai
repository.description: My company AI

commit.message:
Add --reset flag to seed script for clean test state

reset_test_decks() fetches all deck names, filters for anything starting
with 'TestDecks', sorts deepest first, and deletes with cardsToo=True.
Catches intermediate parent decks (TestDecks::Programming etc.) that
Anki auto-creates alongside leaf decks.

Also fixes idempotent re-run: add() now swallows AnkiConnect duplicate
errors so running without --reset is safe and skips existing cards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
